title: Regression and Gradient Descent
description: Fitting a line to data with linear regression, the mean squared error loss, and how gradient descent learns the weight and bias by following the loss downhill.
objectives: Describe the linear regression model y = w x + b and its residuals
Compute the mean squared error (MSE) loss for a candidate line and explain why it is minimised
Explain gradient descent as repeatedly nudging w and b downhill using the MSE gradients, with the learning rate as step size
Compute the MSE gradients with respect to w and b by hand for a small dataset
Trace one or more gradient-descent update steps by hand, computing exact gradients, updated weights and loss
Interpret the slope and intercept of a fitted line in the context of the data

lessons.title: Fitting a line: the model and the MSE loss
Gradient descent: following the loss downhill
A worked gradient-descent trace by hand
